message:
ci(coverage): bump job timeout from 30 to 45 minutes

The coverage job previously short-circuited at the compile step, so a
30-minute window was sufficient. After the RUST_MIN_STACK and llvm-cov
fixes in 942c8d70/fb2a65b9, it now runs through compile plus the full
~3500-test suite under llvm-cov instrumentation (2–3× slower than plain
`cargo test`), which can push past the old 30 min cap on a cold cache.

Raise the timeout to 45 minutes to match the `test-rust` matrix job in
the same workflow file, eliminating the spurious "timeout" failures
that started appearing after coverage began actually completing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -39,7 +39,12 @@ jobs:
   coverage:
     name: Test Coverage
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
-    timeout-minutes: 30
+    # llvm-cov instrumented tests take noticeably longer than plain `cargo
+    # test` (2-3x). Previously the job short-circuited at the compile step
+    # so 30 min sufficed; now it gets through compile + ~3500 tests, which
+    # can push past 30 min on a cold cache. Match CI's `test-rust` matrix
+    # timeout (45 min) for safety.
+    timeout-minutes: 45
 
     steps:
       - name: Checkout repository
